introduction.p: At the local hardware store, Rae bought a hammer and four boxes of nails. The total cost was {\$60}\text{.} The hammer costs {\$35} more than a box of nails. How much does a hammer cost and how much does one box of nails cost?

statement.p: Write two equations that form a system for this scenario.

solution.p: Since the total cost of the hammer and four boxes of nails is {\$60}\text{,} one equation is {x+4y = 60}\text{.}
Since the hammer costs {\$35} more than a box of nails, another equation is {x = y+35}\text{.}

statement.p: Plot the lines for the system of two equations.

image.shortdescription: a Cartesian grid with two intersecting lines; one line passes through (0,60/4) and (60,0); the other line passes through (0,-35) and (35,0); the lines cross at (40,5)

statement.p: Based on the graph, how much does the hammer cost?
How much does a box of nails cost?

solution.p: The lines cross at (40,5)\text{.} So a hammer costs {\$40}\text{,} and a box of nails costs $5.
